headline: Free parties in France come under increasing surveillance
subhead: Under an upcoming bill, which is set to be debated in the Assemblée Nationale on Monday, prison sentences would apply not only to the organizers of these self-managed techno parties, but also to their attendees. Protest parties have taken place across France in response to this crackdown.

UK's Starmer reportedly set to quit as Labour rival sworn in as MP
What are prediction markets, the new form of sports betting promoted by the football World Cup?
subhead: Online platforms known as prediction markets, a form of largely unregulated online betting, have seen rapid growth, fueled in particular by the surging popularity of sports betting.

headline: Jean Pisani-Ferry: 'France's top priority will be to regain control of its public and welfare finances'
subhead: Before stepping down, the governor of the Bank of France, in a letter to President Emmanuel Macron, listed France's shortcomings. Economist Jean Pisani-Ferry welcomes this approach in his column.

headline: Mysterious whale 'necropolis' discovered at the bottom of the Indian Ocean
subhead: A team of researchers has uncovered hundreds of fossilized whale carcasses off the southwest coast of Australia. Spanning 1,200 kilometers, this is the largest, deepest and oldest cetacean bone graveyard ever observed.

headline: Branko Milanovic, economist: 'There will be no return to neoliberal ideology'
subhead: Asian shock, Western decline and the rise of national-liberalism: The expert on global inequality analyzes the fractures of modern capitalism in an interview with Le Monde.

What is included in France's comprehensive law against gender-based and sexual violence?
subhead: Following the death of 11-year-old Lyhanna in early June, momentum is growing for a comprehensive law to protect women and children, combining prevention and prosecution measures.

headline: The uncertain fate of non-Russian prisoners of war captured by Ukrainian forces
subhead: Hundreds of foreign fighters held in captivity in Ukraine find themselves in legal limbo, as Moscow refuses to include them in prisoner exchanges.
byline: Emmanuel Grynszpan,  Jedrzej Nowicki  and  Thomas d’Istria

headline: Le Monde's point-by-point breakdown of the fragile US-Iran framework agreement
subhead: After more than 100 days of war, the United States and Iran signed a memorandum of understanding aimed at ending their conflict. The document will be followed by negotiations to address issues it does not resolve. Le Monde publishes the full text, along with context and analysis.
